mission.title: apisix: feature:  prometheus plugin `apisix_http_status` metric  `route` tag Improve recognition
mission.description: feature:  prometheus plugin `apisix_http_status` metric  `route` tag Improve recognition. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current apisix deployment
steps.description:
Verify your apisix version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
helm list -n apisix 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working apisix installation.

steps.title: Review apisix configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant apisix configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get configmap -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=apisix
```
### Issue description
metric like this:
apisix_http_status{code="200",route="00000000000000000030",service="",node="10.30.30.35"} 
The value of the route tag needs to be changed to the real route value instead of route id.
and add host field.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feature:  prometheus plugin `apisix_http_status` metric …
steps.description:
Under the condition of setting `uris` or `hosts`, I want to record the specific elements in the `uris` or `hosts` that match the current request.
At first I tried to take advantage of the pass by reference feature of the table, so I wrote the following code
```
    handler = function (api_ctx,
```yaml
handler = function (api_ctx, curr_req_matched_record)
        api_ctx.matched_params = nil
        api_ctx.matched_route = route
        --to keep the record which matches the current request and pass it to api_ctx
        api_ctx.curr_req_matched_record = curr_req_matched_record
    end
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get events -n apisix --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feature:  prometheus plugin `apisix_http_status` metric …" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Under the condition of setting `uris` or `hosts`, I want to record the specific elements in the `uris` or `hosts` that match the current request.
codeSnippets:
handler = function (api_ctx, curr_req_matched_record)
        api_ctx.matched_params = nil
        api_ctx.matched_route = route
        --to keep the record which matches the current request and pass it to api_ctx
        api_ctx.curr_req_matched_record = curr_req_matched_record
    end

match_opts.method = api_ctx.var.request_method
    match_opts.host = api_ctx.var.host
    match_opts.remote_addr = api_ctx.var.remote_addr
    match_opts.vars = api_ctx.var

    match_opts.matched = {}
    local ok = uri_router:dispatch(api_ctx.var.uri, match_opts, api_ctx)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with apisix installed or the issue environment reproducible.
